name: Onboard new hires with Asana and Telegram end to end

parameters.text: =✅ Onboard new hires with Asana and Telegram end to end — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf08622-task`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Task Payload

### Normalize Task Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming task records to a stable schema

Next: Route by Work Type

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: employee, contractor + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'employee' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'contractor' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'employee' → Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Task Tracker', matched on `taskId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'contractor' + Alert

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Appends a new row to 'Task Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Telegram

### Escalate Failure to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

# Onboard new hires with Asana and Telegram end to end

**Pattern:** Onboarding Sequence · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Onboard new hires with Asana and Telegram end to end. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual task routing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Task Payload → Route by Work Type → Process 'employee' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Save Execution Metadata → Tag Pipeline Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to onboarding sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating task routing in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your task routing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
